title: Today's AI Brief: The SpaceX IPO is great for Elon Musk and terrible for you
summary: A look at the SpaceX IPO, a website bucking the AI trend, and more

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your AI briefing. I've got a few stories to share today that caught my eye. First up, the SpaceX IPO. You might have heard it's happening, and it's making headlines for all the right – or wrong, depending on how you look at it – reasons.

The SpaceX IPO is being hailed as a massive success for Elon Musk, and it's hard to argue with that. But as Elizabeth Lopatto points out over at The Verge, it might not be so great for the rest of us. The piece that really stuck with me is how this fits into the larger narrative of 'financial nihilism.' Essentially, it's the idea that money and wealth are becoming so detached from the rest of us that it's almost like a different game altogether.

Next, I came across a story about Craig Campbell, a founder who's bucking the trend by focusing on what some might call 'the old school web.' He's creating a website, and it's actually paying off. In 2022, he walked away from a 'blank check' to start this venture, and it's intriguing to see how that's playing out.

Also on my radar is the ongoing conversation around AI, data boundaries, and trust. While not directly related to these stories, it's an essential part of the AI landscape. As we move forward, it's interesting to consider how these factors will influence the development and adoption of AI technologies.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
